topic: T-LINUX-X86 thread-CPU route freeze (OBJ-SIMPLIFY-TIMERS M1.G1 row 2)
tach_path_under_test: src/arch/thread_cpu_linux_inline.rs — PERF_COUNT_SW_TASK_CLOCK read via the perf mmap page using CAP_USER_TIME (time_mult/time_shift + a raw TSC read), with the raw CLOCK_THREAD_CPUTIME_ID syscall as fallback
verdict_detail: Selection is gated on the runtime host capability cap_user_time. When present (bare metal), the inline perf task-clock mmap read is available and ~33x faster than the syscall and self-check-correct, so it is availability-preferred. When absent (Nitro VMs) the raw CLOCK_THREAD_CPUTIME_ID syscall is used by necessity. No same-target speed flip: perf wins whenever the capability is present; the syscall is used only when it is not.

environments.selected_route: perf task-clock mmap (availability-preferred, ~33x faster and self-check-correct)
